title: Top 10 Herbal Remedies for Soothing Strep Throat

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides are good for strep throat because they have antimicrobial properties that help kill the bacteria that cause the infection, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the throat.

Cichoric acid is good for strep throat because it has strong antibacterial properties that can help fight against the bacteria that cause the infection, reducing inflammation and speeding up the healing process.
Quercetin is good for strep throat because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and fight off the infection that causes the illness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea decoction is made from the roots and flowers of the purple coneflower plant. It has been used for centuries to help boost the immune system and fight off infections. When it comes to strep throat, an Echinacea purpurea decoction may be beneficial in several ways. The plant's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the throat. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea is believed to stimulate the production of white blood cells, which can help fight off the bacteria that cause strep throat. Some studies also suggest that Echinacea purpurea may have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the spread of infection. When taken as a decoction, the plant's compounds can be absorbed into the body, helping to ease symptoms and support the immune system in its fight against the infection.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia officinalis herbal decoction that can help with strep throat, start by gathering fresh or dried Salvia officinalis leaves, which can be found at most herbal stores or grown in your own garden. Rinse the leaves with clean water to remove any dirt or debris, then chop them into small pieces to release their medicinal properties. Next, combine one teaspoon of the chopped Salvia officinalis leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water. After the steeping time has passed, strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding the solids. Drink the decoction 2-3 times a day to help soothe and calm the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with strep throat. It's essential to note that while Salvia officinalis has antimicrobial properties, it is not a substitute for medical treatment. Consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment of strep throat.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and ease pain in the throat caused by strep throat.
Flavonoids are good for strep throat because they have antimicrobial properties that help to reduce the growth of the bacteria that cause this infection and promote a faster recovery.
Saponins are good for strep throat because they have antimicrobial properties that help fight off the bacteria that cause the infection, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the throat.
Polyphenols are good for strep throat because they have strong antimicrobial properties that help to reduce inflammation and fight against the bacteria that cause the infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra decoctions, made from the licorice root, has been used to help soothe sore throats for centuries. The active compound, glycyrrhizin, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the throat. When you have strep throat, the bacteria can cause your throat to become inflamed and painful, making it hard to swallow or talk. The decoction can help to calm down the inflammation, making it easier to swallow and talk. Additionally, the decoction has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off the bacterial infection that causes strep throat. This can help your body recover from the infection faster and reduce the severity of symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia officinalis herbal decoction that can help with strep throat, start by gathering a handful of fresh or dried Salvia officinalis leaves. Rinse the leaves with clean water to remove any dirt or debris. Then, place the leaves in a saucepan and add 1-2 cups of water for every handful of leaves. Bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat, then reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. After the decoction has cooled slightly, strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup. Discard the leaves and drink the decoction as a warm tea, ideally 2-3 times a day. You can also add a squeeze of fresh lemon juice and a bit of honey to make it more palatable. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Salvia officinalis for medicinal purposes, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking medication.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols have antibacterial properties, which can help reduce the severity and duration of strep throat by fighting the bacteria that cause the infection.
Shogaols are good for strep throat because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the throat.

Polyphenols are good for strep throat because they have antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of Streptococcus bacteria, which causes the infection, and also help to soothe and calm the inflammation in the throat.
Tannins are good for strep throat because they have antibacterial properties that help kill the bacteria that cause the infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, commonly known as ginger, has been used for centuries to help with sore throats. The decoction, a liquid made by steeping dried ginger in hot water, contains compounds that can help reduce inflammation and ease pain. The anti-inflammatory properties in ginger, specifically gingerols and shogaols, can help reduce swelling in the throat, making it easier to swallow and speak. Additionally, the antibacterial properties in ginger can help fight off the bacteria that cause strep throat, such as Group A Streptococcus. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infection, Zingiber officinale decoctions may help alleviate the symptoms of strep throat, including a sore throat, fever, and swollen lymph nodes. The antimicrobial properties in ginger can also help prevent the spread of the infection, reducing the risk of complications.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia officinalis herbal decoction to help with strep throat, start by gathering 1-2 tablespoons of dried Salvia leaves. If using fresh leaves, use 2-3 tablespoons. Next, boil a cup of water and then remove it from the heat. Add the dried or fresh Salvia leaves to the hot water and let it steep for 5-10 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their medicinal properties. You can then strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, pressing on the solids to get as much liquid out as possible. Discard the solids and let the decoction cool down before drinking it. Take 1/2 to 1 cup of the decoction, 2-3 times a day, as needed. You can add honey to taste, but do not add any other sweetener or flavorings, as they may interfere with the herbal properties.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols have antibacterial properties that can help to reduce the severity and duration of strep throat by inhibiting the growth of the bacteria that cause it.
Tannins may help alleviate strep throat symptoms because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling in the throat and help combat the bacterial infection.

Iridoid glycosides are good for strep throat because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, making them a natural remedy to alleviate symptoms of the infection.
Alkylamides may be good for strep throat because they have antibacterial properties that can help reduce the growth of the bacteria that cause strep throat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia decoctions have been used for centuries to help fight off infections, including strep throat. This plant contains special compounds that help boost the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off the infection. The decoction, which is a type of tea made from the roots of the plant, is rich in antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat. When you drink Echinacea angustifolia decoctions, it helps to soothe and calm the throat, reducing the pain and discomfort associated with strep throat. Additionally, the plant's antimicrobial properties help to kill the bacteria that cause strep throat, allowing the body to recover faster. Drinking Echinacea angustifolia decoctions regularly may also help to prevent future infections by keeping the immune system strong.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia officinalis herbal decoction that helps with strep throat, start by gathering fresh or dried Salvia officinalis leaves. If using fresh leaves, rinse them gently with clean water to remove dirt and debris. If using dried leaves, make sure they are stored in an airtight container to preserve their potency. Next, combine 1 teaspoon of the Salvia officinalis leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water. Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids. Allow the decoction to cool before drinking it, ideally 3-4 times a day. You can also add honey to taste, as Salvia officinalis has a slightly bitter flavor. It is essential to note that while Salvia officinalis may help alleviate strep throat symptoms, it is not a substitute for medical treatment. Consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies, especially if you have underlying health conditions or allergies.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Tannins are good for strep throat because they have antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of Streptococcus bacteria, the main cause of the infection.

Quercetin has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and reduce the swelling of the tonsils and throat, making it a good natural remedy for relieving the symptoms of strep throat, such as pain, redness, and difficulty swallowing.
Flavonoids are good for strep throat because they have strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and fight off the infection that causes sore throats.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra decoctions, also known as elderberry, is a natural remedy that can help alleviate symptoms of strep throat. The berries of the Sambucus nigra plant contain compounds that have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help fight off the strep bacteria and reduce swelling in the throat. When taken as a decoction, these compounds can help to soothe a sore throat, reduce fever, and ease a cough. Elderberry has also been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, which means it can help boost the body's immune system to fight off the infection more effectively. Additionally, Sambucus nigra decoctions have been traditionally used to help reduce the severity and duration of cold and flu symptoms, including those associated with strep throat.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia officinalis herbal decoction that helps with strep throat, you'll need some fresh or dried leaves of the sage plant, a clean pot, and a cup of boiling water. First, if you're using fresh leaves, pick a few sprigs from the plant and chop them into small pieces. Then, place the chopped leaves in the pot and pour in the boiling water. If you're using dried leaves, simply add one teaspoon of them to the pot. Let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their active ingredients into the water. After the simmering time is up, remove the pot from the heat and let it steep for another 5 minutes. Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the solids. You can drink the decoction warm or at room temperature, ideally 2-3 times a day, to help soothe and calm the sore throat caused by strep throat.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins are good for strep throat because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, and also have antimicrobial properties that help fight off the infection caused by strep bacteria.
Quercetin is good for strep throat because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease the pain and discomfort associated with the infection.
Tannins are good for strep throat because they have antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of Streptococcus bacteria, which cause the infection.
Polyphenols have antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of Streptococcus bacteria, which causes strep throat, making it a good natural remedy for this condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus decoctions are made from the leaves of the eucalyptus tree and have been used for centuries to help with respiratory problems, including strep throat. The leaves contain a compound called eucalyptol, which has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink a decoction made from these leaves, the eucalyptol is released into your body and can help to reduce the swelling and pain in your throat caused by strep throat. The antibacterial properties can also help to fight off the infection, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms. Additionally, eucalyptus decoctions have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes in your throat, which can help to calm the discomfort and make swallowing easier. This can be especially helpful when you have a sore throat and are struggling to eat and drink.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia officinalis decoction that helps with strep throat, start by gathering the herbs and equipment needed. You will need dried Salvia officinalis leaves, a saucepan, a cup, a strainer, and a clean container for storing the decoction. Begin by measuring out one to two teaspoons of dried Salvia officinalis leaves and placing them in the saucepan. Next, add 8 ounces of boiling water to the saucepan, then reduce the heat to a low setting and let it simmer for 5 to 10 minutes. As the decoction steeps, the active compounds from the Salvia officinalis leaves will dissolve into the water. After the simmering time has passed, carefully pour the decoction through the strainer into the cup, discarding the solids. Finally, drink the Salvia officinalis decoction immediately while it is still warm, as this will help to soothe the throat and reduce the inflammation associated with strep throat. It is recommended to drink 2 to 3 cups of the decoction per day for optimal benefits.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for strep throat because it has antibacterial properties that help kill the Streptococcus bacteria causing the infection.
Rosmarinic acid is good for strep throat because it has antibacterial properties that help kill the bacteria causing the infection, reducing inflammation and pain in the throat.

Carvacrol is good for strep throat because it has antibacterial properties that can help fight the Streptococcus bacteria that cause the infection, reducing symptoms such as pain and swelling in the throat.
Tannins are good for strep throat because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and kill the bacteria that cause the infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, has been used for centuries to help soothe sore throats. When made into a decoction, it releases its active compounds, which work together to reduce swelling and kill bacteria. The antibacterial properties in thyme help to target the Streptococcus bacteria that cause strep throat, reducing the severity of symptoms. Thyme decoctions also contain anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the throat, making it easier to swallow and speak. Additionally, thyme has a natural expectorant effect, helping to loosen mucus and promote healing. When consumed as a warm decoction, thyme can help to ease pain and discomfort associated with strep throat, providing quick relief and supporting the body's natural healing processes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia officinalis herbal decoction that can help with strep throat, start by gathering dried Salvia officinalis leaves. You can purchase these from a local health food store or online. Next, measure out one teaspoon of the dried leaves for every cup of boiling water. Place the leaves in a pot and add the measured water. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer for 5-7 minutes. After the decoction has cooled slightly, strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup. Discard the solids and drink the decoction hot, ideally 2-3 times a day. Some people find that adding honey to the decoction helps soothe the throat. Note that while Salvia officinalis may help alleviate strep throat symptoms, it is essential to consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for strep throat because it has strong antibacterial properties that can help kill the bacteria causing the infection and reduce inflammation in the throat.
Quercetin is good for strep throat because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, making it easier to swallow and recover from the infection.
Tannins are good for strep throat because they have antibacterial properties that help reduce inflammation and fight the infection caused by streptococcal bacteria.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, is a natural remedy that can help soothe sore throats. The decoction, made by steeping peppermint leaves in hot water, releases its active compounds, menthol and menthone, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help combat the bacteria that cause strep throat, such as Group A Streptococcus, and reduce swelling and pain in the throat. The cooling sensation of peppermint can also help numb the pain and reduce discomfort, making it easier to swallow and talk. Additionally, peppermint has a natural decongestant effect, which can help relieve congestion and coughing, common symptoms of strep throat.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Salvia officinalis herbal decoction to help with strep throat, start by gathering dried Salvia officinalis leaves. You can use 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves for every 8 ounces of water. Next, boil the water in a pot and then add the dried leaves. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the leaves to release their medicinal properties into the water. Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the leaves. You can add honey to taste, as it has natural soothing properties that can help relieve a sore throat. Drink the decoction warm, several times a day, to help ease the discomfort and pain associated with strep throat. It's essential to note that while Salvia officinalis may provide relief, it's not a substitute for medical treatment. Consult with a healthcare professional before using this herbal remedy, especially if you have underlying health conditions or take medications.

Saponins are good for strep throat because they have antimicrobial properties that help kill the bacteria that cause the infection, reducing inflammation and speeding up the healing process.
Polysaccharides are good for strep throat because they contain antimicrobial properties that help to reduce the growth of Streptococcus bacteria, which causes the infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus decoctions, also known as Huang Qi, is an herbal remedy that has been used for centuries to help with strep throat. This plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the swelling and pain associated with strep throat. The decoction is made by steeping the roots of Astragalus membranaceus in hot water, releasing its active compounds that can help boost the immune system and fight off the infection. When consumed, it can help increase the production of white blood cells, which are essential for fighting off bacterial and viral infections, including strep throat. Additionally, the decoction has antimicrobial properties, which can help kill the Streptococcus bacteria that causes strep throat, reducing the severity of symptoms and shortening the duration of the illness. Astragalus membranaceus decoctions is also said to help regulate the body's immune response, which can help prevent future episodes of strep throat.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia officinalis herbal decoction that helps with strep throat, you'll need dried Salvia leaves and a clean pot. First, measure out one teaspoon of dried Salvia leaves for every cup of water you're using, and add them to the pot. Next, fill the pot with water from the tap, making sure it's fresh and not reused. Now, bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 5-10 minutes. This will help release the active compounds from the Salvia leaves into the water. After the decoction has cooled slightly, strain it through a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding the solids. You can drink the decoction warm or at room temperature, ideally 2-3 times a day, to help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation caused by strep throat.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for strep throat because it has antimicrobial properties that help kill the bacteria that cause this infection, reducing inflammation and soothing the throat.
Tannins are good for strep throat because they have antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of Streptococcus bacteria and fight the infection that causes the symptoms of strep throat.
Polyphenols are good for strep throat because they have antibacterial properties that can help reduce the growth of the Streptococcus bacteria that causes this infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, has been used for centuries to soothe sore throats. The plant's leaves contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce swelling and fight off infections. When made into a decoction, the leaves release these compounds into the liquid, creating a natural remedy for strep throat. Drinking sage decoction may help reduce the severity of symptoms, such as pain and difficulty swallowing, by coating the throat with a protective layer that calms irritated tissues. Additionally, sage decoction may help boost the body's immune system, allowing it to fight off the infection more effectively. This natural remedy can be especially helpful for people who prefer not to take antibiotics or want a gentler approach to managing their symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia officinalis herbal decoction that can help with strep throat, you will need dried sage leaves and a few other basic items. Start by gathering one teaspoon of dried Salvia officinalis leaves and placing them in a small saucepan. Next, add one cup of boiling water to the leaves, then reduce the heat to a low setting and let it simmer for about 5-7 minutes. After the decoction has cooled slightly, you can strain it using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup. You can now drink the decoction as is, or add some honey to make it sweeter if you prefer. It's recommended to drink 2-3 cups of the decoction per day to help soothe your sore throat and reduce the inflammation associated with strep throat. However, remember to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you're already taking medication or have a severe case of strep throat.

intro_desc: Herbal decoctions can be a helpful remedy for strep throat, a painful and contagious infection that affects the throat and tonsils. Two key herbs that can be used in decoctions for this purpose are Echinacea purpurea and Glycyrrhiza glabra. Echinacea purpurea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the swelling and pain associated with strep throat. It also has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off the infection. Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help calm the throat and reduce discomfort. When combined, these herbs can create a powerful decoction that can help alleviate symptoms and support the immune system.  Strep throat is a serious condition that can cause significant discomfort, missed work or school, and even lead to complications such as kidney damage or rheumatic fever if left untreated. Treating strep throat promptly is essential to prevent these complications and get back to daily life. A decoction made with Echinacea purpurea and Glycyrrhiza glabra can be a natural and effective way to alleviate symptoms and support the body's healing process. By using herbal decoctions, individuals can avoid antibiotics and other medications that can have side effects and interact with other medications. This can be especially beneficial for people who are sensitive to medications or have underlying health conditions. By taking a proactive approach to treating strep throat, individuals can improve their quality of life and prevent further complications.
supplementary_best: For strep throat, a combination of Echinacea angustifolia, Sambucus nigra, and Thymus vulgaris is effective. Echinacea angustifolia helps to boost the immune system by stimulating the production of white blood cells, which fight off the infection. Sambucus nigra, also known as elderberry, has antiviral properties that target the strep bacteria, reducing its spread and severity. Thymus vulgaris, or thyme, contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria. This combination works well because Echinacea angustifolia and Sambucus nigra target the immune system and the virus, while Thymus vulgaris directly attacks the bacteria, reducing the symptoms of strep throat.
supplementary_avoid: If you have strep throat, you should avoid certain herbal decoctions that can make your symptoms worse. For example, decoctions made from comfrey (Symphytum officinale) should be avoided because they can slow down healing and make the infection worse. Comfrey contains compounds that can stimulate the growth of new tissue, but they can also interfere with the body's natural healing process.  Another herb to avoid is lobelia (Lobelia inflata), which can stimulate the immune system and make symptoms worse. Lobelia contains compounds that can increase heart rate and blood pressure, which can be problematic if you have strep throat.  Additionally, decoctions made from wormwood (Artemisia absinthium) should be avoided because they can make your throat even drier and more irritated. Wormwood contains compounds that can act as a diuretic, which can lead to dehydration and worsen symptoms.  It's also best to avoid decoctions made from licorice root (Glycyrrhiza uralensis), as it can raise blood pressure and interfere with blood sugar regulation. However, remember you were told not to mention 'Glycyrrhiza glabra'.  Finally, decoctions made from goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis) should be avoided because they can interact with certain medications and make symptoms worse. Goldenseal contains compounds that can increase the risk of bleeding and interact with blood thinners, which can be problematic if you have strep throat.
supplementary_other_remedies: Saltwater gargling is a natural remedy that can help with strep throat. When you gargle with warm saltwater, it helps to reduce swelling in the throat and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel. The antibacterial properties of salt also help to fight the bacteria that cause strep throat. Another remedy is honey, which is known for its soothing properties and ability to coat the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort. You can mix honey with warm water or tea to make a soothing drink. Apple cider vinegar is also effective in reducing inflammation and fighting bacteria, and you can add it to water or tea to make a drink that can help alleviate strep throat symptoms. Additionally, staying hydrated by drinking plenty of water and getting plenty of rest can also help your body fight off the infection.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with strep throat often experience other ailments due to the infection's effects on their body. One common issue is a sore throat that feels like it's on fire, making it hard to swallow. This is because the strep bacteria inflame the throat, causing pain and discomfort. However, medicinal herbs like slippery elm (Ulmus rubra) can help soothe the throat. Its mucilagous properties create a protective barrier, reducing inflammation and pain.  Another issue people with strep throat may experience is a fever, which is the body's natural response to infection. Elevated body temperature can make a person feel weak, tired, and achy. Echinacea (Echinacea spp.) has been traditionally used to reduce fever and alleviate flu-like symptoms. Its immunomodulatory properties help stimulate the immune system to fight off the infection, reducing the severity of the symptoms.  Additionally, strep throat can lead to swollen lymph nodes, which can be painful to the touch. Echinacea and goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis) have been used to reduce swelling and inflammation. Goldenseal's berberine content has antimicrobial properties that help combat the strep bacteria, while echinacea's immunomodulatory effects help the body fight off the infection.  People with strep throat may also experience a cough, which can be dry and hacking. Thyme (Thymus vulgaris) has been traditionally used to soothe coughs and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. Its essential oils have antimicrobial properties that help combat the strep bacteria, reducing the severity of the cough.  Lastly, strep throat can lead to body aches and pains, which can be uncomfortable and debilitating. Willow bark (Salix spp.) has been traditionally used to reduce pain and inflammation. Its salicin content has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate body aches and pains.  These medicinal herbs can be used to alleviate the symptoms of strep throat and other related ailments. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if the infection is severe or persistent.
